agency: New York City Department of City Planning (DCP)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, respecting robots.txt — nyc.gov disallows only /html/misc/). Fingerprinted the shared nyc.gov/Akamai agency site, the Netlify-hosted Planning Labs apps (ZoLa zola.planning.nyc.gov, Population FactFinder), the CARTO + Mapbox map stack, the subscription-gated GeoClient/GeoService at api.nyc.gov/geo (401), and the NYCPlanning GitHub org (308 repos). Open Data agency label 'Department of City Planning (DCP)' verified via the Socrata Discovery API; all 188 DCP assets pulled and column-inspected for PLUTO, NTAs, community districts, census tracts, and the zoning tax-lot DB.
headline_findings: DCP is the SOURCE of the city's shared geography — PLUTO/MapPLUTO defines the BBL, and DCP defines community districts, NTAs, census tracts, and council/election-district boundaries that Parks, DOE, Council, and BOE datasets all join on. This makes DCP the natural authoritative base for the project's planned nyc-commons shared schemas.

It has the most real engineering of any domain so far — a subscription-gated GeoClient/GeoService (Geosupport) on Azure API Management, modern Netlify/CARTO/Mapbox map apps (ZoLa, FactFinder), and 308 open-source repos — yet still no single owned, resource-oriented, agent-native DCP API.

188 DCP Open Data datasets (PLUTO 108 cols, MapPLUTO, zoning tax lots, community districts, NTAs, census tracts, borough/election geographies, facilities, POPS) — the richest geography catalog in the city, but flat assets plus GitHub release files, not a resource model.

The canonical address->BBL geocoder is reachable only through a subscription-gated, Azure-hosted GeoClient (api.nyc.gov/geo, 401 to our client) — the single highest-value capability, un-owned as a public contract.

DCP is a REFERENCE agency, not a transactional one: there is no obvious citizen write-workflow. Its ULURP land-use process is an agency/applicant pipeline, not a resident transaction. The net-new value is a canonical geocode contract, not a write endpoint.
See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md for the platform + API inventory.

apis_observed.note: Canonical address->BBL/BIN + administrative-geography resolver; the city's most-referenced geo capability, behind a vendor gateway + API key.
188 DCP datasets — PLUTO/MapPLUTO, zoning tax lots, community districts, NTAs, census tracts, geographies; flat assets, not a resource model.
Public lot/zoning/geography map front-end; canonical zola.planninglabs.nyc.
308 repos: db-pluto, labs-zola, labs-factfinder, geosupport-docker; build side released as files, not served as an API.

fruit.name: Tax Lots (PLUTO / MapPLUTO)
opendata_match: Primary Land Use Tax Lot Output (PLUTO) 64uk-42ks (108c)
Primary Land Use Tax Lot Output - Map (MapPLUTO) f888-ni5f
NYC Zoning Tax Lot Database fdkv-4t4z (16c)
fruit.notes: THE source of the BBL and the citywide geography spine. 108 columns per lot including land use, zoning, FAR, owner, and every administrative district. Belongs as GET /lots/{bbl}.

fruit.name: Zoning Districts
opendata_match: NYC Zoning Tax Lot Database fdkv-4t4z (16c)
fruit.notes: Residential/commercial/manufacturing base districts + overlays + special districts. Encoded per-lot in PLUTO; mapped in ZoLa.

fruit.name: Community Districts
opendata_match: New York City Population By Community Districts xi7c-iiu2 (8c)
Demographic/Social/Economic/Housing Profiles by Community District kvuc-fg9b
fruit.notes: 59 DCP-defined Community Board areas — a primary administrative geography the whole city references.

fruit.name: Neighborhood Tabulation Areas (NTAs)
opendata_match: Census Demographics at the NTA rnsn-acs2
fruit.notes: DCP-defined aggregations of whole census tracts for reliable neighborhood demographics; the neighborhood geography analysts reference.

fruit.name: Census Geographies (2020 tracts)
fruit.notes: DCP-maintained, shoreline-clipped census tracts; the finest grain of the spine, with parent NTA + CDTA.

fruit.name: Council & Election District boundaries
fruit.notes: DCP publishes the City Council + election-district geographies every Council/BOE dataset joins on. DCP is the source, not the consumer.

fruit.name: Geocode (address -> BBL + geography spine)
fruit.notes: The canonical address->BBL/BIN + administrative-geography resolver, behind Azure API Management + a subscription key (401 to our client). No open twin. The single highest-value NET-NEW owned contract (read) — DCP has no citizen WRITE workflow.

fruit.name: Land-Use Applications (ULURP)
fruit.machine_readable: ZAP project pages + Housing DB pipeline
fruit.notes: The closest DCP has to a transaction, but it is an agency/applicant process (developers, Community Boards, CPC, Council) — NOT a citizen write-workflow. Cross-references the Council domain's matterId when actions reach the City Council.

fruit.name: Facilities Database
fruit.notes: Citywide inventory of public/institutional facilities keyed to BBL — another DCP spine product other agencies reuse.
